rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF FELIX M. BUCKBERG MICHAEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Opening A Blocked Account And Exparte
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): No proof of opening blocked accounts has been filed. No ex parte petition for order depositing funds in blocked accounts has been filed. Estate funds have been held in unblocked accounts since 2010 without bond. Guardians have been withdrawing funds, even though they are not authorized to take possession of money or any other property without specific court order as so stated in letters and appointment order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW GEORGE JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 7 status reports have been filed since October 2015, the initial deadline for closing the estate. No accounts have been filed. Now that the Club's 850 petition has been disposed of, it is unclear what else needs to be done to close the estate. The current status petition requested an extension of administration of this estate until December 2020. The petition has not been amended to request additional time. The estate has less than $5,000, despite the inventories and appraisals showing the total value exceeding $170,000. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TITO DANILO VARGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminal Formal Proceedings; And For Authority To Administer Estate Pursuant To Probate Code Section 7660(A)(1)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The statutory authority for termination of formal administration, though not cited in the petition, is presumably Probate Code section 12251, combined with a petition to re-appoint the public administrator as personal representative of summary administration under section 7660(a)(1). Section 12251(b) requires notice to be given to all interested persons. No petition for an order determining the heirs entitled to distribution has been filed, while the two accounts covering the 5-year period have already been approved, with a 73.5% allowance of statutory compensation taken by the administrator and her attorney. No final account of formal administration has been or is proposed to be presented before the estate assets on hand are transferred to the public administrator as personal representative of summary administration. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021. 1) Please provide legal authority/probate code sections in support of the requests in the petition and explain how the legal authority/probate code sections apply. There appears to be no legal authority for the request for a second preliminary distribution. 2) Please explain provide a legal basis for the court issuing an order "authorizing petitioner to complete the settlement with the former tenants of estate real property by paying the sum of $125,000.00 to such tenants" when there is already a 1/30/2020 court order that says "administrator is authorized to pay the balance of the settlement amount ($125,000.00) to defendants pursuant to the modified stipulation for entry od judgment upon close of escrow for sale of 61 capp street by the estate. 3) Issue for eventual hearing: whether to order a 2-year account under Probate Code 10950. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANE QUEEN GAINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The executor distributed $21,517.50 to two adult beneficiaries without court approval. The executor has not taken any recovery action against those beneficiaries to rectify the unauthorized preliminary distributions. The executor has not kept the estate funds in interest-bearing accounts ever since the estate received distribution from the decedent's mother's trust in or about September 2020. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK P PUESCHEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021. 1) Petitioner has not filed proof of satisfaction for the Verizon Wireless creditor claim. 2) Please explain what the Bank of America claim was for. Please explain and provide a legal basis for paying $395,180.63 when the creditor claim was for $385,454.89 and was allowed for $385,454.89. 3) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)] The order must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SARAH A. NERAD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021. 1) Please explain if any hidden jewelry was found in the house after the occupant vacated the home on 05/03/2021. 2) Please explain as it appears the 07/07/2021 Final I&A for $500.00 is not included in this account or petition and it appears an amendment or amended petition needs to be filed. 3) Please explain on Schedule A entries 3/1/21 and 4/1/21 for "Western Conference of Teamsters Pension Trust (March 2021) for $993.21 these appear to may be duplicate entries. 4) Please explain on Schedule A the 2 entries that appear to be duplicates for 2/10/21 Social Security for $979.00. 5) Please provide a receipt for entry on Schedule C 3/4/21 GreatCall - service (including upgrades for smartphone/data) $158.50. 6) The Bank Statement for Chase does not verify the balance at the end of the account as the account ends 4/11/2021 and the bank statement ends 4/13/2020. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 7) The Bank Statement submitted for First Republic Bank does not verify the balances as there is no balances indicated on the statement. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 8) Please explain and provide evidence in support of all the alleged liabilities. 9) Please separate any and all entries regarding scanning/digitizing of documents in the conservator of the estate and conservator of the person timesheets so the court can determine how much of each entry is for scanning/digitizing and please provide a legal basis for the conservator billing for scanning/digitizing documents. [LRSF 14.58 B.] 10) Please provide a legal basis for conservator paying for mileage and tolls of the eviction counsel when tolls and mileage are absorbed as part of fees. [LRSF 14.58 B.] 11) Please provide a legal basis for conservator paying for interest on eviction counsel's invoices. [LRSF 14.58 B.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021. no response to examiner notes filed. 1) Petitioner must file notice of the petition. Matter continued for proper notice. 2) Please provide the court order appointing James C. Meeks is administrator of the estate of Frank B. Simmons as the petition alleges James C. Meeks is Administrator of the Estate of Frank B. Simmons and it appears James C. Meeks has not been appointed as administrator. 3) The petition must have an attachment 7. (This is different from attachment 7a and 7b. See petition page 1 paragraph 7.) Petition does not conform to LRSF 14.26. 4) Please file evidence showing James C. Meeks is a spouse of Decedent. 5) A copy of the current vesting deed must be filed for any real property. [LRSF 14.26]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN H ROCKWELL III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021 1) Please explain. Page 2 of the petition alleges there is no real property and $1,000,000.00 in personal property. The 7/26/2021 Supplemental Declaration alleges Petitioner is currently residing in the home of the Decedent and plans to continue to live in the home for an extended period of time. It appears the petition must be amended, re-noticed, and re-published as there appears to be real property that is not included in the petition. [CRC 7.53]. 2) Please provide a legal basis for residing in Decedent's real property and overseeing repairs and remodeling of the home. 3) Court to determine bond at the hearing. Petitioner has a NC driver license but alleges she is a CA resident and resides in Decedent's home and plans to live in the home for an extended period of time. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STUART SELIGSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether the declaration of due diligence is sufficient or whether more needs to be done.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE COHOLAN FAMILY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 14, 1990 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders 1) For A Verified Accounting And Report And To Produce Records; 2) To Compel Distribution; 3) For Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fi
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
